fact: May 27 is exactly 147 days into the year - that's almost exactly 1/3 of the way through!
In the Northern Hemisphere, this is prime time for baby birds to be learning how to fly and crash-landing in people's backyards.
The sun rises before 6 AM in most northern cities by this date, making it one of the earliest sunrise times of the year.
May 27 falls smack in the middle of Gemini season - the zodiac sign known for being chatty and having split personalities.
In Australia and other Southern Hemisphere places, this date marks the heart of autumn when leaves are turning brilliant reds and golds.
Dandelions are going absolutely crazy around this time, turning into those white puffball seed heads that kids love to blow.
We're only about 3 weeks away from the summer solstice, so daylight is stretching longer every single day in the north.
Late May is when many trees finish dropping their helicopter seeds - those spinning maple seeds that twirl down like tiny tornadoes.
In cold mountain areas, this is when the last stubborn patches of snow finally give up and melt into rushing streams.
